key.description: Identifier used for accessing specific environments from code.

area.description: The area the environment should apply in.

description.description: Optional description of what this changes or why.

isPohTheme.description: Whether the environment is for a player-owned house theme.

isUnderwater.description: Whether the environment is for an underwater area, such as Guardians of the Rift.

force.description: Whether to force the environment to apply, even if atmospheric lighting is disabled.

allowSkyOverride.description: Whether to allow overriding the sky with default sky.

lightningEffects.description: Whether the area should have flashing lightning effects.

allowRoofShadows.description: Whether the area should allow roof shadows to be drawn. Defaults to true.

instantTransition.description: Whether transitions to and from this area should always be instant.

cycleMode.description: Forces the day/night cycle mode for this environment, overriding the player's config setting.

ambientColor.description: Ambient light color in sRGB, specified as a hex color code or an array in the range 0-255. Accepts decimals.

ambientStrength.description: Ambient light strength multiplier. Defaults to 1.

directionalColor.description: Directional light color in sRGB, specified as a hex color code or an array in the range 0-255. Accepts decimals.

directionalStrength.description: Directional light strength multiplier. Defaults to 0.25.

waterColor.description: Water color in sRGB, specified as a hex color code or an array in the range 0-255. Accepts decimals.

waterCausticsColor.description: Water caustics color in sRGB (as hex or array). Defaults to the environment's directional light color.

waterCausticsStrength.description: Water caustics strength. Defaults to the environment's directional light strength.

underglowColor.description: Underglow color in sRGB (as hex or array). Acts as light emanating from the ground.

underglowStrength.description: Underglow strength multiplier. Acts as light emanating from the ground.

moonColor.description: Moon color in sRGB, specified as a hex color code or an array in the range 0-255. Controls the visible moon disk and its glow, and (unless moonLightColor is set) the light it casts. Defaults to a cool silver (~8000K).

moonLightColor.description: Color of the light the moon casts on the scene, in sRGB, as a hex color code or an array in the range 0-255. Lets moonlight be tinted differently than the visible moon disk. When omitted, falls back to moonColor.

nightSkyColor.description: Color the night sky (zenith and horizon) is tinted toward as the moon rises, in sRGB, as a hex color code or an array in the range 0-255. Lets the night sky color be set independently of the moon disk. When omitted, falls back to moonColor.

sunAngles.description: The sun's altitude and azimuth specified in degrees in the horizontal coordinate system. Altitude is from the ground up, azimuth is clockwise from north.

fixedSunAngles.description: Locks the sun disk and its shadow direction to a fixed altitude and azimuth (degrees) while a fixed cycle mode (FIXED_DAWN/FIXED_MIDDAY/FIXED_SUNSET/FIXED_NIGHT/ALWAYS_NIGHT) is active, overriding the astronomically-derived position. Altitude is from the ground up (negative places the sun below the horizon), azimuth is clockwise from north. Ignored in DYNAMIC mode.

fixedMoonAngles.description: Locks the moon disk and its shadow direction to a fixed altitude and azimuth (degrees) while a fixed cycle mode is active, overriding the default fixed-night moon position. Altitude is from the ground up, azimuth is clockwise from north. Ignored in DYNAMIC mode.

fogColor.description: Sky/fog color in sRGB, specified as a hex color code or an array in the range 0-255. Accepts decimals.

fogDepth.description: Fog depth normally ranging from 0 to 100, which combined with draw distance decides the fog amount. Defaults to 25.

groundFogStart.description: Only matters with groundFogOpacity > 0. Specified in local units (1 tile = 128 units), where zero is the lowest height, and larger negative numbers are further up. Defaults to -200.

groundFogEnd.description: Only matters with groundFogOpacity > 0. Specified in local units (1 tile = 128 units), where zero is the lowest height, and larger negative numbers are further up. Defaults to -500.

groundFogOpacity.description: Ground fog opacity ranging from 0 to 1, meaning no ground fog and full ground fog respectively. Defaults to 0.

windAngle.description: Angle in degrees for the wind direction. Zero is east and positive rotates towards north. Defaults to 0.

windSpeed.description: Controls wind turbulence; the rate of change in the wind noise. Larger values cause more rapid changes. Defaults to 15.

windStrength.description: Strength of the displacement caused by wind; the maximum amount a single vertex is allowed to move. Defaults to 0.

windCeiling.description: The height where the `windStrength` will be reached. The closer objects are to this height, the stronger the wind will be. Defaults to 1280 (10 tiles in height).

sunriseSunsetStrength.description: How strongly the procedural day/night sunrise/sunset is allowed to paint this area's sky (0 to 1). 1 = full procedural sunrise/sunset (default). Lower values hold the sky at the area's own regional (fogColor) color through the twilight window and fade the procedural sun glow, so a vividly-colored area (e.g. a blood-red sky) doesn't turn orange/blue at sunrise/sunset. Independent of sunStrength; affects only the sky gradient and sun glow, not daytime or nighttime colors.

skyColorTakeoverAngle.description: Sun altitude in degrees at which this area's own sky color (fogColor) has fully taken over from the procedural sunrise/sunset gradient as the sun climbs. Lower values pull the area color in earlier in the morning (and hold it later in the evening), compressing the procedural twilight; higher values let the procedural gradient persist further up the sky. Applies to all areas regardless of sunriseSunsetStrength. Defaults to 40.

auroraVisibility.description: Aurora visibility multiplier for this area (0 to 1), controllable independently of starVisibility. Auroras still only appear on the randomly-selected aurora nights and fade with the night; this scales how visible they are when they do. When omitted it falls back to starVisibility (the original behavior, where hiding stars also hides auroras). Set it explicitly to decouple: 0 hides auroras while keeping stars, 1 shows full auroras even where stars are dimmed.
